pr.title: Fix some sdata2 placement from recent change
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Moved several .sdata2 constants between or within handwritten assembly files under asm/melee/lb to correct section placement after a recent change. The PR relocates lbl_804D7A58/lbl_804D7A5C/lbl_804D7A60 from code_8000CE50.s into code_8000B074.s, and moves lbshadow.s constants lbl_804D7B30 through lbl_804D7B50 from before the .data section to after the file's .data contents. There was no PR body or review discussion in the provided slice, so the rationale is inferred from the title and diff: preserving expected .sdata2 ordering/placement for matching.
what_changed: Added a .section .sdata2 block at the end of asm/melee/lb/code_8000B074.s containing lbl_804D7A58, lbl_804D7A5C, and lbl_804D7A60.
Removed the same lbl_804D7A58/lbl_804D7A5C/lbl_804D7A60 .sdata2 constants from asm/melee/lb/code_8000CE50.s, leaving lbl_804D7A68 as the first shown .sdata2 symbol there.

In asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s, moved the .sdata2 block for lbl_804D7B30 through lbl_804D7B50 from immediately after code near lbl_8000F9D4 to after the file's .data string/NULL entries near lbl_803BA0FC.
No symbol values appear to have changed; the constants are byte-for-byte relocations such as 0x3F800000, 0x38D1B717, 0xC0400000, 0x43300000, and 0x80000000.
smart_moves: Fixed placement by moving whole labeled data blocks rather than editing individual references or changing constant values, minimizing behavioral risk.
Kept the global labels and raw .4byte encodings intact, preserving existing references while adjusting linker/section ordering.
Separated .data and .sdata2 ordering explicitly in lbshadow.s, which helps maintain the expected layout when assembling mixed code/data assembly files.
decomp_lessons: For matching, small-data section order is as important as the constant values; misplaced .sdata2 labels can break the binary layout even when all symbols and bytes are present.
When splitting or moving assembly between files, verify that .sdata2 constants land in the same relative output order as the original binary.
Do not assume nearby code ownership is sufficient evidence for where a small-data symbol belongs; placement may need to follow linker/object ordering discovered from the original binary.
A safe correction for data-order regressions is to move complete labeled blocks between assembly files while preserving labels, alignment, and .4byte payloads.
naming_conventions: Unidentified labels are kept in address-derived form such as lbl_804D7A58, lbl_804D7B30, lbl_8000D2E0, and lbl_803BA0FC.
Assembly files in this slice are named by subsystem plus starting address or library area, e.g. asm/melee/lb/code_8000B074.s, asm/melee/lb/code_8000CE50.s, and asm/melee/lb/lbshadow.s.
Global data labels in .sdata2 are declared with .global followed by the matching lbl_ address label.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use explicit .section .sdata2 directives to control small-data placement in handwritten assembly.
Relocate unchanged .sdata2 constant blocks between assembly files to correct output layout without changing generated code.
Preserve paired 64-bit-style constants as adjacent .4byte entries, as seen for lbl_804D7B48 with two zero words and lbl_804D7B50 with 0x43300000/0x80000000.
Keep .data and .sdata2 blocks ordered deliberately inside assembly files; lbshadow.s now places the moved .sdata2 block after its .data block.
searchable_terms: Fix some sdata2 placement from recent change
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that convert asm/melee/lb/code_8000B074.s, code_8000CE50.s, or lbshadow.s to C and check how these .sdata2 constants were named or represented.
Search for additional fixes mentioning sdata2 placement or small-data ordering to identify recurring linker-layout rules.
Inspect build map or linker ordering around lbl_804D7A58 through lbl_804D7B50 if future changes disturb nearby .sdata2 layout.
